author: Steve Griffing PE(CSE) CISSP-ISSEP CPP
rev_m_delta_note: Rev M upgrades all 8 PocketBeagle 2 (AM6232) nodes to PocketBeagle 2 Industrial (AM6254). Hardware change only — propulsion, ESCs, cape PCBs, and governor firmware are all unchanged from Rev L. The 8 OS microSD cards are eliminated; each PB2-I boots from its 64GB onboard eMMC. The 4 Cape-B write-blocked log microSD cards are retained.
hardware_reference: bom_revK.json — all hull, propulsion, nacelle, power, payload, sensor, wiring, and radio items are identical to Rev K/L. See bom_revK.json for full part-level detail.

removed.desc: microSD card 32GB (OS boot) — one per node
removed.reason: PocketBeagle 2 Industrial includes 64GB eMMC — OS boots from eMMC, microSD not required

replaced.new_desc: PocketBeagle 2 Industrial (AM6254) — FC node, Cape-A mounted
replaced.notes: FC1–FC4. Quad A53 provides 2× CPU headroom vs dual-A53 AM6232. M4F + PRU-ICSS governor firmware unchanged. Cape-A 72-pin header compatible.

replaced.new_desc: PocketBeagle 2 Industrial (AM6254) — CN node, Cape-B mounted
replaced.notes: CN1–CN4. Same SoC as FC nodes. Cape-B 72-pin header compatible. 64GB eMMC used for OS and GCS log staging.

unchanged: CAPE-A — 85×55mm 4L PCB (FC nodes) — same 72-pin expansion — no redesign
CAPE-B — 90×60mm 4L PCB (CN nodes) — same 72-pin expansion — no redesign
MICROSD-LOG — 4× Cape-B write-blocked flight log microSD (Cape-B nodes only)
All EDF, ESC, nacelle, hull, wiring, payload, sensor, radio items — see bom_revK.json

cost_delta_usd.note: Effectively cost-neutral upgrade. Industrial grade, double RAM, 64GB eMMC, industrial temp range for <$10 additional cost vs standard PB2.

mass_delta_g.note: PB2-I at 13g × 8 = 104g (BOM rounds 12.7g to 13g) vs 10g × 8 = 80g standard. OS microSD removal -8g. Net +16g — negligible impact on T/W.

avionics_nodes.desc: PocketBeagle 2 Industrial (AM6254) — FC node, Cape-A mounted
avionics_nodes.notes: FC1–FC4. Runs Cape-A sensor/flight-control cape. M4F runs Rev L PID governor firmware unchanged. PRU-ICSS provides 4× DSHOT output channels (DSHOT600/1200 at 1 kHz). Cape-A 72-pin expansion headers — Rev K PCB design fully compatible.

avionics_nodes.desc: PocketBeagle 2 Industrial (AM6254) — CN node, Cape-B mounted
avionics_nodes.notes: CN1–CN4. Same SoC as FC nodes. Runs Cape-B comms/payload cape. Cape-B 72-pin expansion headers — Rev K PCB design fully compatible.

avionics_nodes.desc: Cape-A PCB 85×55mm 4L — Sensor + Flight Control cape
avionics_nodes.notes: Unchanged from Rev K. ICM-42688-P IMU, BMP388 baro, M10Q GPS, ATA6561 CAN FD, MAX3485E RS-485, DS26LV31/32+xfmr 1553 PRU, DP83825I×2 ETH PHY, SLB9670 TPM2, 8×PWM servo rail, ESC telem UART. Stacks on PB2-I P1/P2 headers — fully compatible.

avionics_nodes.desc: Cape-B PCB 90×60mm 4L — Comms + Logging + Payload cape
avionics_nodes.notes: Unchanged from Rev K. SiK 915MHz, RFM95W LoRa, TI WL1837MOD WiFi/BT, RCRS-49 header, ATA6561 CAN FD, MAX3485E RS-485, DS26LV31/32+xfmr 1553 PRU, DP83825I×2 ETH PHY, SLB9670 TPM2, ATF16V8BQL CPLD write-blocker, microSD log slot, W25Q128JV NOR flash, DRV8833 winch, HX711 load cell, 2×servo cargo. Stacks on PB2-I P1/P2 headers — fully compatible.

avionics_nodes.desc: RCRS-49 sub-module PCB — 49MHz TDDS RC transceiver

avionics_nodes.notes: One per Cape-B (CN nodes only). Write-blocked by Cape-B ATF16V8BQL CPLD latch. Non-executable, append-only log. Retained in Rev M — these are the tamper-evident flight records, separate from OS microSD which is now eliminated.

avionics_nodes.desc: W25Q128JV 128Mbit SPI NOR flash — circular log buffer
avionics_nodes.notes: On Cape-B. Fast circular write buffer for high-rate sensor telemetry. Unchanged from Rev K.

rev_l_firmware.desc: Rev L PID governor firmware binary — M4F coprocessor partition (unchanged in Rev M)
rev_l_firmware.target: AM6254 M4F coprocessor on all 4 FC nodes (Cape-A: FC1–FC4)
rev_l_firmware.compatibility_note: Binary is fully compatible with AM6254 M4F — no recompile required. AM6254 M4F is same ARM Cortex-M4F architecture as AM6232. Governor logic unchanged.
rev_l_firmware.flash_method: CN1 GCS upload → Ethernet ring → TPM 2.0 verified flash on each FC node

performance.dry_note: Rev L 3197g + 24g PB2-I upgrade (13g×8=104g vs 10g×8=80g) − 8g OS microSD removed = +16g net
performance.rev_m_notes: Rev M replaces 8× PocketBeagle 2 (AM6232) with 8× PocketBeagle 2 Industrial (AM6254). Propulsion, ESCs, capes, and governor firmware unchanged from Rev L. Weight increase +16g (negligible). T/W changes from 3.12 to 3.11 — within spec.

board_swap_steps: Power down. Disconnect all data cables.
Seat 8× PocketBeagle 2 Industrial (AM6254, P/N 100003007) onto same Cape-A/B headers.
Flash OS image to eMMC on each board via USB-C (no microSD needed — eMMC is the boot device).
Remove all 8 OS microSD cards from node slots. Retain 4× Cape-B write-blocked log microSD cards.
Power on. Confirm all 8 nodes boot from eMMC (check /proc/mtd or journalctl for eMMC boot).
Upload governor_firmware_revL.bin via MAVLink to CN1 (unchanged — binary compatible with AM6254 M4F).
CN1 distributes to FC1–FC4 via Ethernet ring (RSTP secure channel).
Each FC node validates binary signature against TPM 2.0 endorsement key.
FC1–FC4 flash M4F partition and confirm via CAN FD heartbeat.
Reboot all FC nodes. Confirm GOV_STATE = NORMAL on all 4 ESCs via MAVLink.
installation_procedure.note: Cape-A and Cape-B PCBs are unchanged. The 72-pin expansion headers are identical between PB2 and PB2-I — no soldering required.

build_notes.rev_m_vs_rev_l: Rev M requires board swap only. All other hardware is unchanged from Rev L/K. No ESC re-programming, no governor re-tuning, no Cape PCB modifications.
build_notes.os_microsd_elimination: The 8 OS microSD cards can be removed after eMMC OS flash is confirmed on all nodes. These are not needed for flight operations. Keep them as recovery media offline.
build_notes.log_microsd_retention: The 4 Cape-B write-blocked log microSD cards MUST be retained. They are the primary tamper-evident flight data recorders and are required for post-flight analysis and regulatory compliance.
build_notes.industrial_temp_benefit: PB2-I rated to −40°C to 85°C. Standard PB2 was 0–70°C. This eliminates thermal throttling risk at high ambient temperatures (hot summer flight ops) and enables cold-weather operations.
build_notes.eMMC_benefit: 64GB eMMC provides ~8× more onboard storage vs typical 8GB OS microSD. This enables longer mission logs to be stored locally before offload.
